channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: iFixit Trump phone teardown confirms it’s an HTC dupe
item.description: After getting its hands on a Trump phone and tearing it apart, iFixit has confirmed what I first reported back in February: the T1 Phone is an almost exact duplicate of the HTC U24 Pro. iFixit partnered with NBC to get hold of the network's media sample of the Trump phone, along with a U24 […]

item.title: La Garmin Forerunner 965 chute sous les 500 € et reste le meilleur plan pour les sportifs
item.description: Alors que sa remplaçante est arrivée sur le marché, la Garmin Forerunner 965 n'a rien perdu de sa superbe pour les amateurs de course à pied. Cette montre de sport profite même d'un prix plus attractif : 499 euros au lieu de 650 euros.

item.title: Fiido Air Carbon Fiber Electric Bike Review: A Light, Quiet Ebike
item.description: This impressively light carbon commuter makes switching to an ebike easier than ever.

item.title: Why encryption alone is not enough in modern communications
item.description: While E2EE protects message content, modern threat actors are no longer attempting to defeat it. Instead, they are exploiting what surrounds it.

item.title: Welcome to the Waymo World Cup
item.description: It might not feel all that different from older World Cups—for better or worse.

item.title: YouTube brings back DMs after 6 years without them
item.description: Six years after YouTube kicked direct messages to the curb, the platform is bringing them back.

item.title: Fable will feature over 150,000 lines of dialogue, Playground Games confirms — 'We've had multiple studios set up running in parallel for over 1000 hours just this year alone, just recording VO'
item.description: Playground Games has said that more than 150,000 lines of dialogue have been recorded for Fable.

item.title: 'Celebrating this partial restoration is not right' — Iran emerges from 88-day internet shutdown, but what happens next?
item.description: As connectivity began to be restored, internet experts have warned that a cutoff "will happen agains." TechRadar talked to Mahsa Alimardani at WITNESS about what this partial restoration actually means, and what could happen next.

item.title: Daily Shows Desi Lydic roasts Melania Trumps latest speech about AI
item.description: "Daily Show" host Desi Lydic has reacted to Melania Trump's latest speech about AI.

item.title: The factory floor ran out of people, and no hiring strategy will fix it
item.description: Manufacturers face permanent worker shortages, accelerating shift to AI-driven, robot-integrated operations.

item.title: I'm genuinely impressed by Sihoo’s 'spaceship-like' ergonomic chair — and not even Amazon beats this Prime Day sale price
item.description: The ultra-adjustable Doro C300 Pro V2 wowed us with "how comfortable the spaceship-like chair felt, even on long days."

item.title: Deezer now helps users find AI music on other streaming platforms
item.description: Deezer will now help you find AI slop in your music playlists even if you're on another platform.

item.title: Deezer just launched a free site to scan your playlists for AI slop — and yes, it works on Spotify, Apple Music and Tidal
item.description: Deezer's new AI music detector scans playlists on any music streaming service to check for slop — but if you want the tracks named, shamed and nixed, you'll need to make the switch

item.title: YouTube is introducing DMs (again)
item.description: YouTube is reintroducing private messaging after testing new ways for users to share videos and "have conversations about them" last year. In an announcement on its official blog, YouTube says it's now starting to expand the in-app video sharing and messaging feature to users in the US and "other global regions" who are 18 or […]

item.title: J’ai mis dix ans à oser Home Assistant : il m’a fallu quelques heures et une IA
item.description: Dix ans que Home Assistant me faisait peur. Quelques heures pour le monter, parce que cette fois, je ne l'ai pas fait seul.

item.title: 'That gap is significant': Lots of us still know nothing about data centers, new survey finds
item.description: Despite their increasingly important role in daily life, many people still have no idea what data centers actually do, report finds.

item.title: Le MacBook Ultra bel et bien dans les tuyaux ? Ces 3 indices de macOS 27 nous laissent flairer sa piste
item.description: Pressenti pour la fin 2026 ou le début 2027, le MacBook Ultra est encore très officieux. Au sein de macOS 27 Golden Gate, trois indices nous mettent néanmoins, très discrètement, sur sa piste.

item.title: Plus de 1 000 chevaux directement dans les roues : Mercedes lance la production de son moteur de tous les records
item.description: Dévoilé en 2025, le nouveau moteur à flux axial de Mercedes va démarrer sa production. Fort de 1 000 chevaux en pic pour seulement 12,7 kilos, ce prototype prépare le terrain des prochaines AMG électriques.

item.title: Sosh sort un nouveau forfait mobile pas cher, et la télé comprise change la donne
item.description: À 13,99 euros par mois, ce forfait Sosh aligne 150 Go en France sur le réseau d'Orange, sans engagement. L'offre ajoute 25 Go pour l'étranger et glisse Orange TV dans le lot, ce qui la rend plus complète que la moyenne à ce tarif.

item.title: NYT Strands hints, answers for June 11, 2026
item.description: The NYT Strands hints and answers you need to make the most of your puzzling experience.

item.title: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for June 11, 2026
item.description: Connections is a New York Times word game that's all about finding the "common threads between words." How to solve the puzzle.

item.title: Wordle today: Answer, hints for June 11, 2026
item.description: Here's the answer for "Wordle" #1818 on June 11 as well as a few hints, tips, and clues to help you solve it yourself.

item.title: Qui va gagner la Coupe du Monde 2026 ? On a posé la question à 5 IA, voici leurs pronostics
item.description: La Coupe du Monde 2026 débute ce 11 juin. Avant le coup d’envoi du premier match, on a demandé à cinq intelligences artificielles de prédire l’intégralité de la compétition, des phases de groupes jusqu’à la finale. Notre objectif est de comparer leurs pronostics et de mesurer, au fil des rencontres, laquelle se montrera la plus fiable.

item.title: Trump’s Border Crackdown Is Wreaking Havoc on the World Cup
item.description: Travel bans and other visa issues are creating problems for World Cup participants even before the whistle blows.

item.title: Cameras, Sensors, and 3D Body Scans: All the Tech Helping Eliminate Blown Calls
item.description: Soccer officials already rely on cameras to see who’s offside and who sent the ball out of bounds. But during this World Cup, refs will use digital twins of each player to view plays from every angle.

item.title: How Mexican World Cup Stadiums Achieved FIFA’s Environmental Certifications
item.description: Venues hosting the 2026 World Cup must meet high standards to obtain environmental certifications, but FIFA also requires that they use natural grass, which is water-intensive to maintain.

item.title: How to Watch the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The games start June 11 and end with a grand finale in New Jersey on July 19. There are 104 of them. Here’s how to watch ’em all.

item.title: Inside the painstaking, yearslong process of making perfect grass for the World Cup
item.description: Researchers spent years developing the grass seed mix, soil depth, and strength of the grasses that will make up the 2026 World Cup pitches. The 2022 FIFA World Cup hadn’t even started when researchers set out to grow the perfect pitch for the 2026 World Cup, which kicks off in cities across North America this month.

item.title: Menopause is burning up senior-level women’s careers. 4 things companies can do to keep them from burning out
item.description: More than 30 women reveal how their menopause and perimenopause symptoms have impacted their professional lives—and their fixes. Kacy Fleming was presenting in a boardroom when the burning started. It began at her thighs and rose up through her torso as the legal counsel in the room pushed back on her points. She whipped off her blazer even though all she was wearing underneath was a threadbare James Perse tank top....

item.title: There’s a hidden cost to working remotely. It’s one employees won’t want to ignore
item.description: New research shows work from home has unexpected mental health effects. As more companies require employees to return to the office five days a week, new research shows working from home may not be so great for your mental health.

item.title: 5 ways the World Cup ticketing process is a complete design fail
item.description: Annoying glitches, confusing web flows, and subpar infographics: As design refs, we’d issue a red card. The ticketing system for the World Cup has been so bad that California, New York, New Jersey, and Texas are investigating FIFA over reports of issues like false advertising and sky-high prices.

item.title: Air CEO Shane Hegde on why creative teams need a system of record
item.description: The chief exec talked with Fast Company about visual data as the enterprise’s most neglected infrastructure problem, and why AI won’t kill the creative spark. Creative teams are drowning in their own output. According to Air’s internal surveys, the average creative spends 20% of their workday hunting for files: old campaign assets, archived footage, forgotten screenshots. Shane Hegde, CEO and co-founder of the New York-based startup, calls this “search debt,” and he’s spent close to a decade building a solution....

item.title: The 5 best World Cup ads (so far)
item.description: Get hyped before kickoff with Adidas, Nike, Uber Eats, Coors Light, and Brahma. It’s time for kick-off. After years of anticipation for what has been billed the biggest World Cup ever, the 2026 tournament hosted by the U.S., Canada, and Mexico is here. Not only is this year’s competition the biggest in terms of teams playing (48, up from 32), it’s expected to draw five billion viewers over its month-long run. And that’s why advertisers are spending upwards of $10.5 billion globally to get their brands in front of all those eyeballs. ...

item.title: Après six mois de travail, Microsoft Teams redevient fluide
item.description: Pointée du doigt pour sa lenteur et sa consommation excessive de mémoire, Microsoft Teams fait peau neuve. La firme de Redmond semble avoir tenu compte des critiques en dévoilant une version de Teams plus rapide que jamais.

item.title: NYT Connections Sports Edition today: Hints and answers for June 11, 2026
item.description: Connections: Sports Edition is a New York Times word game about finding common sports threads between words. How to solve the day's puzzle.

item.title: Framework delays Laptop 13 Pro shipments by a month
item.description: Framework is pushing back shipments of its new Laptop 13 Pro to address issues with its haptic touchpad and display found in the run-up to mass production.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel has some blunt thoughts about Trump commemorative gold coin
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel has shared his thoughts on Trump launching a new commemorative gold coin.

item.title: Cyberattaques chinoises : avec 1 500 appareils infectés, le botnet JDY prépare le terrain pour des opérations d’espionnage
item.description: Un réseau d'espionnage chinois étend silencieusement son emprise. Le botnet JDY compte désormais plus de 1 500 appareils compromis capables de scanner Internet à la recherche de systèmes vulnérables. Les informations recueillies sont alors communiquées à des hackers mandatés par Pékin, en vue de futures cyberattaques.

item.title: NYT Mini crossword answers, hints for June 11, 2026
item.description: Answers to each clue for the June 11, 2026 edition of NYT's The Mini crossword puzzle.

item.title: People Living Near xAI’s Dirty Data Centers Are Furious About the SpaceX IPO
item.description: Elon Musk is set to make hundreds of billions even as communities in Mississippi and Tennessee are fighting to stop the gas turbines powering xAI's supercomputers.

item.title: The best gay dating apps of 2026: Grindr is still king (even if we sometimes wish it wasnt)
item.description: Don't celebrate Pride Month alone. Here's our unfiltered guide to the best gay dating apps based on extensive testing.

item.title: The Insignia 65-inch Class QF LED 4K QLED TV is down to its best-ever price at Amazon ahead of Prime Day
item.description: As of June 11, the Insignia 65-inch Class QF LED 4K QLED TV is back to its lowest-ever price at Amazon of $299.99. This is 40% off its full price of $499.99.

item.title: Taillé pour le gaming mais aussi pour la productivité, ce laptop MSI avec RTX 5060 et Core 7 passe sous la barre des 1 000 €
item.description: Avec sa RTX 5060, son Intel Core 7 ou encore son grand écran offrant des images fluides, le MSI Venture Pro 15 s'adresse aussi bien aux joueurs exigeants qu'aux professionnels à la recherche d'une machine réactive. Il bénéficie en prime d'une petite réduction chez Boulanger : on peut ainsi le trouver à 999,99 euros au lieu de 1 099,99 euros.

item.title: TikTok : comment retrouver une vidéo déjà vue dans votre historique ?
item.description: Vous souhaitez pouvoir revisionner une vidéo Tiktok, mais impossible de la retrouver après avoir rafraîchi votre fil ? Pas de panique, cette petite astuce va vous aider.

item.title: The Insignia 32-inch Class F20 TV is under $70 for a limited time
item.description: Save 46% on the Insignia 32-inch Class F20 at Amazon.

item.title: Anthropic’s new AI model is powerful, dazzling—and about to get really expensive
item.description: Fable is available to subscribers for now. But its upcoming shift to API-only access shows how quickly frontier AI is moving from ‘all you can use’ to ‘all you can afford.’ Anthropic’s eye-poppingly powerful new model, Fable, is worth testing while you still can. Built by the company behind the Claude chatbot, Fable is the publicly safe version of Mythos, the model Anthropic deemed too dangerous to release just two months ago. To make it available to the average user, Anthropic has introduced stringent guardrails designed to prevent the cutting-edge model from being used for high-risk applicat...

item.title: Starlink : les prix augmentent encore, voici combien vont payer les nouveaux clients
item.description: Starlink demande désormais des frais mensuels aux nouveaux abonnés pour la location du matériel.

item.title: The Beats Solo 4 headphones are $70 off at Amazon for a limited time
item.description: Get the best deal on headphones. Save 35% on the Beats Solo 4 at Amazon.

item.title: If AI transparency rules weaken, enterprise tech teams will inherit the risk
item.description: When AI compliance softens, enterprises don't escape accountability — they absorb it.

item.title: Steam Machine : un sérieux indice pointe vers une sortie en juin
item.description: Officiellement, la Steam Machine est prévue pour l'été 2026. Un document de la FCC pointe vers un lancement dès le mois de juin.

item.title: OpenAI signs major Visa deal — so AI agents will soon be able to make payments and purchases for you
item.description: Visa payments to be integrated within OpenAI's ecosystem, paving the way for future agentic transactions and bookings.

item.title: The Beats Fit Pro just got a $50 discount at Amazon for a limited time
item.description: Get the best deal on Beats. Save 25% on the Beats Fit Pro at Amazon.

item.title: Anthropic backtracks on policy that 'sabotaged' researchers' work
item.description: Anthropic is walking back a hidden policy that researchers say sabotaged their work.

item.title: Rachat de SFR : l’Autorité de la concurrence met déjà en garde Orange, Free et Bouygues Telecom
item.description: Benoît Cœuré, le président de l’Autorité de la concurrence, estime que le rachat de SFR « ne va pas de soi », sans pour autant être automatiquement anticoncurrentiel. Il rappelle néanmoins l’amende de 534 millions d'euros infligée en 2005 à Orange, SFR et Bouygues Telecom pour entente illicite.

item.title: Dyson V16 Piston Animal first impressions: A new apex Dyson vacuum has entered the villa
item.description: Released on June 2, the cordless Dyson V16 Piston Animal is officially stronger than any corded Dyson Ball vacuum. But it has its quirks.

item.title: I Was Scammed Buying GLP-1s Online. I’m Not Alone
item.description: Customers have complained that a telehealth network selling compounded GLP-1s has been ripping them off—even after it had to pay $5 million to clients as part of a settlement with the US government.

item.title: Among Us is full of Crewmate butts. It used to have even more.
item.description: "Among Us" creator Owen Dennis breaks down how the show came to be so butt-focused.

item.title: Heres why you should watch Among Us twice
item.description: "Among Us" creator Owen Dennis breaks down why the show's rewatch factor is so great.

item.title: A Prime Day Apple deals primer: How low prices will go on AirPods, iPads, and MacBooks
item.description: The best early Prime Day Apple deals include record lows on AirPods Pro 3 and M5 MacBook Air. Wait to add any other gadgets to your cart.

item.title: Savage House review: Hosting an important dinner? Prepare for battle.
item.description: "Savage House" is an oozing, gangrenous tale of profligacy, with an outstanding Richard E. Grant and Claire Foy.

item.title: The 10 best Lego sets of 2026 so far, from F1 to Monet and Pokémon
item.description: After tracking Lego releases from the better part of the past year, we put together a list of the top 10 Lego sets that hit the market in 2026.

item.title: Whats new to streaming this week? (June 12, 2026)
item.description: What’s new to streaming for the week of June 12, 2026, on Netflix, Prime Video, Hulu, and more? We've got you covered.

item.title: Build apps and websites faster with this Microsoft secret weapon, now $35
item.description: If you're just learning to code, you need Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2026.

item.title: Looking for guilt-free screen time for kids? This $45 app can help
item.description: Learning can be fun with this lifetime subscription to Pok Pok.

item.title: Dropouts Brennan Lee Mulligan knows he cant win. But could that be about to change?
item.description: Sam Reich reveals a could-be path to an Emmy victory for Dropout's Brennan Lee Mulligan.

item.title: How to watch Mexico vs. South Africa online for free
item.description: How to watch World Cup for free. Live stream Mexico vs. South Africa in the 2026 FIFA World Cup for free.

item.title: How to watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup online for free
item.description: How to watch World Cup for free. Live stream the 2026 FIFA World Cup for free from anywhere in the world.

item.title: The best hookup apps for 2026: I swiped until my thumb hurt
item.description: Are you single? We tested the best hookup apps to help you find a summer fling, situationship, or whatever else you wanna call it.

item.title: Ryanair is under investigation over charging parents to sit with their kids
item.description: European economy airline Ryanair is under investigation in the UK for charging parents mandatory fees to sit with their children. The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) said it was looking into whether the seating fees, which may be charging parents for the airline to meet its child safety and disability‑related obligations, are "unfair" under consumer […]...

item.title: How the UK is tackling industrialized crypto fraud
item.description: Crypto fraud has industrialized. The UK is showing how enforcement can keep up.

item.title: « Votre consommation internet à l’étranger atteint 60 euros » : attention au piège du hors forfait chez ces trois opérateurs
item.description: À quelques semaines des vacances d'été, Cdiscount Mobile, NRJ Mobile et Auchan Télécom ont discrètement modifié leurs conditions générales d'utilisation, notamment autour du hors forfait réalisé à l'étranger. Désormais, la connexion internet n'est plus bloquée lorsque l'enveloppe de data allouée est vide.

item.title: The Braun IPL Silk Expert Pro 5 has dropped to its best-ever price
item.description: Save 13% on the Braun IPL Silk·Expert at Amazon.

item.title: Il connecte son Whoop à son agenda pour classer ses collègues les plus stressants
item.description: Un développeur a relié son bracelet Whoop à son agenda pro pour savoir quel collègue lui fait monter le rythme cardiaque. Résultat : un classement des réunions les plus stressantes.

item.title: Tesla : la conduite autonome FSD autorisée en Belgique, à quand le tour de la France ?
item.description: Après le Danemark, c’est désormais au tour de la Belgique d’autoriser la conduite autonome FSD de Tesla sur ses routes. Le pays devient ainsi le 5ème à approuver cette technologie, qui se rapproche de la France.

item.title: « Trop rapide » : pourquoi la BCE a bloqué les nouveaux produits de Revolut
item.description: La Banque centrale européenne a bloqué pendant des mois le lancement de nouveaux produits de Revolut dans l'Union, au motif que la fintech allait trop vite.

item.title: Windows 11 : Microsoft toucherait enfin à son curseur de souris, inchangé depuis des années
item.description: Microsoft réfléchirait à un nouveau design pour le curseur de la souris dans Windows 11, un élément resté quasi inchangé depuis des années.

item.title: Puissante et confortable, cette trottinette avec moteur 1350 W et suspension intégrale avant/arrière tombe à 629 €
item.description: La Navee ST5 Pro est une trottinette électrique haut de gamme qui succède à la ST3 Pro sortie au printemps 2025, à retrouver en promo pour 629,10 euros au lieu de 799 euros sur Amazon.
